grid_dims_observed.note: Identical grid dimension chain to EE-03 (page 61, ground-floor lighting plan) -- both are the same ground-floor base plan, cross-validating each other and the master grid file.

elements.description: Outlet marked '+1.20M' near the top-right corner of ทานอาหาร
Outlet marked '+1.20M' near the door of เก็บของ
Outlet marked '+1.20M' near the sink/counter area of ครัว

Outlet marked '+1.20M' near the staircase in โถง, with a '3/LP' circuit tag printed immediately beside it -- marking this as the feed point from panel LP circuit 3, matching the 'เต้ารับ' load label recorded on EE-02's panel schedule (page 60)
Outlet marked '+1.20M' near the left wall of พักผ่อน
Outlet marked '+1.20M' near the bottom of พักผ่อน close to the เฉลียง boundary

warnings: GRID: dimension chain matches EE-03 (same ground-floor base plan) and the master grid file exactly.
This is a 2-story house so this sheet only covers the ground floor; the corresponding upper-floor outlet plan is EE-06 (page 64, separate file).

No outlet symbol was confidently identified in ซักล้าง, ห้องน้ำ 1, จอดรถ, or เฉลียง on this crop -- consistent with the same rooms lacking clearly-attributed lighting fixtures on EE-03, may simply be genuinely unequipped rooms (laundry/carport/porch commonly rely on adjacent-room circuits) or symbols too faint to resolve; flagged rather than invented.

Total outlet count summed from this best-effort element list is approximately 7 -- all appear to share the same '+1.20M' standard mounting height and, per EE-01's legend, are likely the plain duplex ⊖ type (not the waterproof ⊟ type) since none are in wet areas on this floor; not independently confirmed which glyph variant each uses at this resolution.

The '3/LP' circuit tag near the staircase cross-validates against EE-02's panel schedule circuit position 3 (RCBO 16AT,1P, load 'เต้ารับ') -- consistent with this being the ground-floor outlet circuit.
A small boxed label '3 | +0.20 | 5' near ห้องน้ำ 1 is an architectural floor-level marker, not an electrical symbol -- excluded from elements, same as noted on EE-03 (page 61).
